190951:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-013.md:9758:- **What it does**: MemPalace exposes many overlapping write authorities: direct drawer writes, diary writes, KG writes, AI-directed hook saves, and optional hook auto-ingest. 197079:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:7924:### Finding 4: Public should reject a separate L0/L1/L2/L3 engine and express wake-up behavior as profiles on the existing retrieval stack
197084:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-012.md:8009:### Finding 4: Public should reject a separate L0/L1/L2/L3 engine and express wake-up behavior as profiles on the existing retrieval stack
197146:I’ve got enough to synthesize cleanly now. The final cut is converging on a small, stable set: one clear adopt-now item, a couple of prototype/presentation ideas, one true new-feature track, and one broad rejection where the metaphor costs more than it earns.
197164:FINAL SYNTHESIS: Classify EVERY finding as adopt-now / prototype-later / new-feature-needed / reject. Executive summary.
197166:MemPalace contributes two near-term ideas worth carrying into Public now: a compaction-time structured save checkpoint and a short bootstrap hint layer that teaches memory behavior. Everything else should stay scoped: selective verbatim evidence retention and layered wake-up are worth prototyping, a temporal fact plane is a separate net-new feature track, and full taxonomy plus AAAK/diary-centric recall should be rejected as primary architecture. I used prior phase memory only to recover continuity; the findings below are based on current-turn source reads.
197168:## Findings
197169:### Finding 1: Pre-compaction preservation is the clearest adopt-now transfer
197173:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
197176:### Finding 2: Status-as-protocol is worth porting as a tiny bootstrap hint layer
197180:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
197183:### Finding 3: Raw-verbatim upside is real, but only as selective evidence retention
197187:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
197190:### Finding 4: L0-L3 wake-up is a presentation pattern, not a storage model to copy
197194:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
197197:### Finding 5: A separate temporal fact plane is the only true net-new feature track
197201:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
197204:### Finding 6: AAAK and diary-centric memory should stay out of the core recall path
197208:- **Recommendation**: reject
197211:### Finding 7: Full palace taxonomy is still the wrong center of gravity for Public
197215:- **Recommendation**: reject
197245:- New information ratio: 0.84
197253:## Recommended Next Focus
197269:FINAL SYNTHESIS: Classify EVERY finding as adopt-now / prototype-later / new-feature-needed / reject. Executive summary.
197271:MemPalace contributes two near-term ideas worth carrying into Public now: a compaction-time structured save checkpoint and a short bootstrap hint layer that teaches memory behavior. Everything else should stay scoped: selective verbatim evidence retention and layered wake-up are worth prototyping, a temporal fact plane is a separate net-new feature track, and full taxonomy plus AAAK/diary-centric recall should be rejected as primary architecture. I used prior phase memory only to recover continuity; the findings below are based on current-turn source reads.

198692:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:12:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
198695:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:17:- **What it does**: Engram gives agents obvious lifecycle verbs: `mem_session_start`, `mem_session_end`, `mem_session_summary`, `mem_context`. Public instead centers on trusted sessions, `memory_context` mode resolution, `session_resume`, `session_bootstrap`, interrupted-session recovery, and cached-summary validation. In practice, Engram is easier to explain to any MCP client; Public is harder to misuse and better at rejecting stale or untrusted continuity.
198697:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:19:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
198699:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:60:Research Engram's persistent memory architecture, MCP tool design, and session lifecycle patterns to identify practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially for Spec Kit Memory. Focus on how Engram separates agent tools from admin tools, persists session history, stabilizes evolving topics with `topic_key`, and ranks or shortcuts search using SQLite + FTS5. Compare Engram's choices against current Public capabilities and classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`. Prioritize improvements that strengthen session continuity, memory hygiene, search usefulness, and agent-agnostic integration without duplicating systems Public already has.
198701:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-005.md:106:14. 198908:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:9:Research Engram's persistent memory architecture, MCP tool design, and session lifecycle patterns to identify practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially for Spec Kit Memory. Focus on how Engram separates agent tools from admin tools, persists session history, stabilizes evolving topics with `topic_key`, and ranks or shortcuts search using SQLite + FTS5. Compare Engram's choices against current Public capabilities and classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`. Prioritize improvements that strengthen session continuity, memory hygiene, search usefulness, and agent-agnostic integration without duplicating systems Public already has.
198910:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:55:14. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and make sure the report explicitly distinguishes `adopt now`, `prototype later`, and `reject`.

Capture the evidence needed to prove agentId,sessionId,provenanceSource,provenanceActor} metadata 3) Query with matching scope and verify hit appears 4) Query with mismatched user/agent or tenant and verify hit is filtered out 5) Review governance_audit rows. Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.` | 1) Attempt `memory_save()` with `tenantId/sessionId` but missing provenance fields and verify rejection 2) Save with full `{tenantId,userId | agentId,sessionId,provenanceSource,provenanceActor}` metadata 3) Query with matching scope and verify hit appears 4) Query with mismatched user/agent or tenant and verify hit is filtered out 5) Review `governance_audit` rows | Missing provenance rejects governed ingest; successful governed ingest persists scope metadata; mismatched scope cannot retrieve the memory; allow/deny decisions are written to `governance_audit` | Save/search outputs + DB query of scoped columns + audit rows showing allow/deny decisions | PASS: Missing provenance rejected, valid governed save succeeds, cross-scope retrieval returns no hit, and audit rows exist; FAIL: Ungoverned save slips through or cross-scope retrieval leaks data |

198942:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5809:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/022-hybrid-rag-fusion/009-perfect-session-capturing/scratch/legacy-memory-quarantine/15-03-26_12-27__i-m-starting-with-the-repo-s-own-runbook-and.md:535:**Selected**: memory_save({ dryRun:true }) now returns sufficiency results, reasons, and rejectionCode when a memory is under-evidenced.
198943:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5810:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/022-hybrid-rag-fusion/009-perfect-session-capturing/scratch/legacy-memory-quarantine/15-03-26_12-27__i-m-starting-with-the-repo-s-own-runbook-and.md:1009:PLEASE IMPLEMENT THIS PLAN: # Fix Plan for `009-perfect-session-capturing` Workspace Identity and Manual Validation Drift ## Summary Fix `009-perfect-session-capturing` by making the repo-local `.opencode` folder the canonical workspace identity for native session capture, then align backend matching, stateless validation, and manual docs to that contract. This is not a simple “change the root path” patch. The implementation should: - normalize backend-native paths into a shared `.opencode` workspace identity, - stop relying on raw absolute-path equality across CLIs, - prevent false `V7`/`V8` aborts caused by incomplete stateless normalization, - re-baseline `M-007` so it reflects actual shipped behavior instead of stale assumptions. ## Key Changes ### 1. Canonical workspace identity - Add a shared workspace-identity helper that derives the canonical target from the nearest repo-local `.opencode` directory. - The helper should produce an equivalence set for the active workspace, including: - the canonical `.opencode` path, - the enclosing repo root when `.opencode` is nested inside it, - normalized absolute forms used by CLI artifacts. - Replace direct project-root equality checks in native extractors with “same workspace identity” matching. - Keep `.opencode` as the source of truth; repo-root and git-root matches are accepted only when they resolve to the same workspace identity. ### 2. Backend matcher updates - Update `OpenCode`, `Claude`, `Codex`, `Copilot`, and `Gemini` capture discovery to use the shared identity helper before accepting a transcript/workspace/session. - Preserve existing backend-specific safety rules: - exact session selection preference where available, - reasoning/thought stripping, - in-project file filtering, - no cross-workspace opportunistic matching. - Make loader precedence unchanged conceptually, but evaluated after canonical identity normalization. ### 3. Stateless normalization and validation fixes - Fix stateless `TOOL_COUNT` derivation so `V7` is based on actual structured tool evidence, not just `FILES.length`. - Extend normalized capture shaping so read/search/bash-heavy sessions do not render execution artifacts while still reporting `tool_count: 0`. - Tighten prompt/exchange relevance fallback so lack of keyword match does not blindly re-include foreign-spec prompt text and trip `V8`. - Keep contamination blocking strict, but ensure valid captures for the active `.opencode` workspace are not rejected due to fallback pollution. ### 4. Manual validation and documentation truth - Update `M-007` so it explicitly states that native capture targets the repo-local `.opencode` workspace identity. - Rewrite `M-007b`, `M-007c`, and `M-007e` through `M-007i` expectations to match the corrected pipeline: - thin JSON fixture must be defined so it truly falls below threshold if abort is still intended, - alignment scenario must account for earlier gate precedence, - precedence means “first matching backend chosen,” not “first backend always yields a successful save.” - Refresh the feature catalog entry so “exact project match” is restated as “exact canonical workspace identity match via `.opencode` normalization.” - Update spec `010` evidence/acceptance text to separate: - discovery precedence, - capture normalization, - final save/index success. ## Public Interfaces / Types - Add a shared internal workspace-identity utility used by extractors and loader-facing matching. - No CLI signature changes for `generate-context.js`. - No change to JSON-mode authority semantics. - `DataSource` values remain the same. - Matching semantics change internally from raw absolute-path equality to canonical `.opencode` workspace equivalence. ## Test Plan 1. Add focused unit coverage for the workspace-identity helper. - `.opencode` path matches itself - repo root matches the same `.opencode` - git root matches the same `.opencode` - unrelated repo with a different `.opencode` is rejected - normalized symlink/trailing-slash variants remain equivalent 2. Expand extractor tests for each backend. - session accepted when artifact stores repo root but active canonical workspace is `.opencode` - session accepted when artifact stores `.opencode` - unrelated workspace rejected even if basename is similar - thought/reasoning stripping remains intact 3. Expand stateless normalization/validation tests. - `V7` no longer fires for tool-rich captures with sparse `FILES` - `V8` no longer fires because of fallback-to-all-prompt contamination when spec relevance is missing - thin JSON fixture either: - truly aborts below threshold, or - is reclassified as allowed and the docs are updated accordingly 4. Re-run the full closure stack. - `npm run lint` - `npm run build` - targeted Vitest suite for session capture, runtime memory inputs, stateless enrichment, and scorer calibration - JS regression suites - alignment drift - `spec/validate.sh` for `009-perfect-session-capturing` 5. Re-run `M-007` manually against the corrected runtime with no in-memory root override. - `M-007a` rich JSON authority - `M-007b` corrected thin-fixture expectation - `M-007c` mis-scoped stateless scenario - `M-007d` provenance-tagged enrichment - `M-007e` through `M-007i` backend precedence under canonical `.opencode` identity - `M-007j` empty-home `NO_DATA_AVAILABLE` ## Assumptions and Defaults - The user-approved contract is that repo-local `.opencode` is the only canonical workspace identity. - Repo root, git root, and backend-native stored paths are compatibility forms, not primary identifiers. - Documentation should be updated in place in the existing feature catalog entry and `M-007`, not split into new parallel docs. - If thin JSON still scores above threshold after the identity fixes, the playbook must be corrected rather than forcing an artificial abort rule that conflicts with shipped scorer behavior.
198944:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5812:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/022-hybrid-rag-fusion/009-perfect-session-capturing/scratch/legacy-memory-quarantine/15-03-26_12-27__i-m-starting-with-the-repo-s-own-runbook-and.md:1135:PLEASE IMPLEMENT THIS PLAN: # Cross-Platform Memory Sufficiency Hardening ## Summary Add one shared “is this enough context for a proper memory?” gate across the entire save surface, not just native session capture. The new rule is: - good memories save - off-spec memories still fail alignment/contamination as they do now - thin, under-evidenced, or metadata-only memories fail with a new explicit `INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT_ABORT` - this hard-block applies everywhere: native capture, explicit JSON, and MCP `memory_save` This plan keeps `.opencode` workspace identity and the existing fallback order, but stops treating a spec anchor, long rendered output, or auto-generated metadata as sufficient proof of memory quality. ## Key Changes ### 1. Add one shared memory-sufficiency evaluator - Introduce a shared internal module that evaluates a normalized evidence snapshot instead of relying on backend-specific heuristics. - The evaluator should return: - `pass` - `rejectionCode: 'INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT_ABORT'` - `reasons` - `evidenceCounts` - `score` or normalized sufficiency value for diagnostics - Use the same pass contract across all platforms: - specific title is required - at least 1 primary evidence item is required - at least 2 total spec-relevant evidence items are required - semantic substance is required: minimum aligned text floor such as `>=250` semantic chars or `>=40` unique words - Count as primary evidence only: - meaningful file role with non-placeholder description - meaningful tool result tied to the target task/spec - concrete decision / next action / blocker / outcome - substantive observation with a non-generic title and useful narrative - Count as support evidence only: - additional aligned prompt or exchange - anchored section structure - trigger phrases that are not generic - recent context with specific request/learning - Do not count: - workspace match by itself - generic titles like `session`, `development session`, `update` - placeholder file descriptions - single anchored prompts with no other substance - synthetic/provenance enrichment unless it is clearly target-spec-specific ### 2. Harden `generate-context.js` and the native/JSON pipeline - Run the new sufficiency gate after stateless enrichment and relevance filtering, before file writing or indexing. - Apply it to: - `OpenCode` - `Claude Code` - `Codex CLI` - `Copilot CLI` - `Gemini CLI` - explicit JSON input - Keep failure types distinct: - off-spec or same-workspace wrong task: `ALIGNMENT_BLOCK` - foreign-spec dominated content: existing contamination/quality failure path (`V8`) - not enough real context even though aligned: `INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT_ABORT` - Tighten normalization so backend evidence survives when it is real: - preserve meaningful tool outputs - preserve file-role hints - preserve decision/next-step evidence - keep single-message anchored sessions from being inflated into valid memories - Keep the legacy 0-100 scorer for diagnostics only. - Keep v2 `quality_score` metadata, but make it reflect insufficiency strongly so thin memories score low instead of looking healthy. ### 3. Apply the same rule to MCP `memory_save` - Add the shared sufficiency gate to the server-side save path in the common handler flow, not as a session-capture-only rule. - Run order should be: 1. parse/validate 2. quality-loop auto-fixes for recoverable formatting only 3. shared sufficiency gate 4. embedding / dedup / persistence - The quality loop must not try to “repair” semantic insufficiency by inventing context. - `force: true` must not override insufficient-context rejection. - `dryRun: true` must surface the insufficiency result with reasons and evidence counts, with no write/index side effects. - The new insufficiency rejection must hard-block immediately and must not respect the older warn-only rollout behavior from the save-quality gate. ### 4. Align scoring, responses, and docs - Add a stable explicit failure contract everywhere: - CLI/runtime errors include `INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT_ABORT` - MCP save responses include `rejectionCode` and insufficiency details - Update server response builders and recovery hints so the user/agent sees “not enough context for a proper memory” instead of a vague generic quality failure. - Refresh the canonical docs in place: - `009-perfect-session-capturing` for capture-entry behavior - `NEW-139` feature-catalog entry - pre-storage quality gate feature doc - quality-loop feature doc - dry-run preflight feature doc - manual playbook scenarios for both capture and `memory_save` ## Public Interfaces / Types - New shared internal type such as `MemorySufficiencyResult` / `MemoryEvidenceSnapshot`. - New stable failure code: `INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT_ABORT`. - `generate-context.js` CLI signature does not change, but its error contract expands to include the new failure code. - `memory_save` response should add explicit insufficiency reporting, including `rejectionCode` and structured reasons/evidence. - No backend-specific save thresholds; all platforms use the same sufficiency contract after their evidence is normalized. ## Test Plan - Add direct unit coverage for the shared sufficiency evaluator: - rich specific evidence passes - single anchored prompt fails - generic title plus long content fails - placeholder file descriptions do not count - spec-specific tool output can satisfy primary evidence - Expand session-capture tests for: - same-workspace off-spec OpenCode still `ALIGNMENT_BLOCK` - rich Codex capture passes and no longer false-fails - foreign-spec Claude capture still fails the existing contamination path - thin but anchored Copilot and Gemini captures fail `INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT_ABORT` - thin explicit JSON fails `INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT_ABORT` - rich explicit JSON still passes - Expand MCP save tests for: - `memory_save({ dryRun: true })` returns explicit insufficiency result - normal save rejects insufficient memories before embedding/storage - `force: true` does not bypass the new gate - quality-loop fixes formatting issues but cannot rescue semantic insufficiency - warn-only quality-gate behavior does not bypass the new insufficiency hard block - Re-run the relevant automated stacks: - scripts lint/build/Vitest/JS suites - MCP server quality-gate, quality-loop, memory-save, and preflight suites - alignment drift - spec validation - Rework manual testing so it proves: - rich JSON/native saves still pass honestly - thin aligned saves fail explicitly - all five native backends behave consistently - `memory_save` dry-run and real save both notice insufficient context ## Assumptions And Defaults - “All platforms” means all five native CLI backends, explicit JSON input, and MCP `memory_save`. - The new `INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT_ABORT` is immediate and authoritative everywhere. - `force` can bypass dedup/update constraints when already supported, but not alignment, contamination, or insufficient-context gates. - The implementation should reuse existing docs where possible instead of creating parallel feature-catalog entries unless a validator or taxonomy rule requires one.
198945:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5813:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/022-hybrid-rag-fusion/009-perfect-session-capturing/scratch/legacy-memory-quarantine/15-03-26_12-27__i-m-starting-with-the-repo-s-own-runbook-and.md:1177:Analyze this issue spotted by plan Plan: Fix Memory File Generation Quality (Root Cause) Spec folder:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/022-hybrid-rag-fusion/009-perfect-session-capturing Context Generated memory files (output of generate-context.js) have systemic quality issues: missing ANCHOR tags, generic frontmatter trigger phrases, and cross-session contamination. The user's most recent memory save produced an 893-line file with zero ANCHOR tags despite the template containing 26 of them, and frontmatter trigger_phrases that say "memory dashboard" / "session summary" instead of session-specific content. Additionally, when saving from Claude Code (or any external CLI), the script's session capture detects the active OpenCode editor session instead of the calling session, leading to ALIGNMENT_BLOCK rejections or saving the wrong session's context. Root cause investigation traced these to bugs in scripts/core/workflow.ts and templates/context_template.md. --- Root Cause Analysis BUG 1: ANCHOR tags destroyed by HTML comment stripping (CRITICAL) Location: workflow.ts:108,162-179,1360-1364 Chain of events: 1. Template context_template.md contains 26 ANCHOR tags: &lt;!-- ANCHOR:id --&gt; / &lt;!-- /ANCHOR:id --&gt; 2. Step 8 (populateTemplate) renders the template correctly, anchors intact 3. Step 8.5 calls stripWorkflowHtmlOutsideCodeFences() on the rendered output 4. That function uses WORKFLOW_HTML_COMMENT_RE = //g (line 108) 5. This regex matches all HTML comments, including every ANCHOR tag 6. Line 171:.replace(WORKFLOW_HTML_COMMENT_RE, '') — deletes them all 7. Result: 0 ANCHOR tags in output file Irony: Line 538 has extractAnchorIds() that parses ANCHOR tags — but it runs after they've been destroyed. Fix: Modify the HTML comment regex to exclude ANCHOR comments: // Before (line 108): const WORKFLOW_HTML_COMMENT_RE = //g; // After: const WORKFLOW_HTML_COMMENT_RE = //g; The negative lookahead (?!\s*\/?ANCHOR:) preserves &lt;!-- ANCHOR:id --&gt; and &lt;!-- /ANCHOR:id --&gt; while still stripping all other HTML comments. BUG 2: Frontmatter trigger_phrases are hardcoded (HIGH) Location: templates/context_template.md:4-7 vs templates/context_template.md:807-811 Chain of events: 1. Template frontmatter (lines 4-7) has static values: trigger_phrases: - "memory dashboard" - "session summary" - "context template" 2. The  system spec kit/022 hybrid rag fusion/010 perfect session capturing, dry run, rejection code, no data available, memory_save, render quality, re reading, m 007, json mode, repo local, save path, target spec, tool call, current spec, auto fixes, warn only, save quality, under evidenced, hard blocks, post render, home directory, negative path, user selected, tree thinning, implementation summary, malformed generated memory files, system, spec, kit/022, hybrid, rag, fusion/010, perfect, session, capturing mustache variable IS used — but only in the YAML metadata block at line 807-811 (inside the trailing ```yaml code fence) 3. The workflow DOES extract session-specific triggers (preExtractedTriggers, line 1231-1269) and passes them as TRIGGER_PHRASES to the template (line 1315) 4. But the frontmatter section never references  system spec kit/022 hybrid rag fusion/010 perfect session capturing, dry run, rejection code, no data available, memory_save, render quality, re reading, m 007, json mode, repo local, save path, target spec, tool call, current spec, auto fixes, warn only, save quality, under evidenced, hard blocks, post render, home directory, negative path, user selected, tree thinning, implementation summary, malformed generated memory files, system, spec, kit/022, hybrid, rag, fusion/010, perfect, session, capturing — it uses hardcoded strings Fix: Replace the static frontmatter trigger_phrases with the template variable: # Before (lines 4-7): trigger_phrases: - "memory dashboard" - "session summary" - "context template" # After: trigger_phrases: {{#TRIGGER_PHRASES}} - "" {{/TRIGGER_PHRASES}}  BUG 3: Cross-session contamination in direct-path mode (HIGH) Location: workflow.ts — collectSessionData() call path in direct-path mode Chain of events: 1. User runs generate-context.js specs/015-skill-alignment from Claude Code CLI 2. The script's direct-path mode calls collectSessionData() which captures the active OpenCode editor session (e.g., "Media Editor: Convert images to webp") 3. The alignment check compares this unrelated session against the target spec folder 4. Result: 0 file matches, 0 phrase matches → ALIGNMENT_BLOCK error 5. Workaround: user must fall back to JSON file mode (/tmp/save-context-data.json) The JSON fallback produces lower quality because it lacks the rich session data (file diffs, observations, tool calls) that automatic capture provides. This creates a two-tier quality problem: sessions saved from external CLIs are always worse. Fix: When JSON data is provided (Mode 1), skip collectSessionData() entirely — the caller has already provided the session context. The alignment check should only run when using direct-path mode (Mode 2) with auto-captured session data. --- Files to Modify ┌─────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────┐ │ # │ File │ Change │ ├─────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤ │ │ │ Fix HTML comment │ │ 1 │.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts │ regex to preserve │ │ │ │ ANCHOR tags (line │ │ │ │ 108) │ ├─────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤ │ │ │ Replace hardcoded │ │ 2 │.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/templates/context_template.md │ frontmatter │ │ │ │ trigger_phrases with │ │ │ │  system spec kit/022 hybrid rag fusion/010 perfect session capturing, dry run, rejection code, no data available, memory_save, render quality, re reading, m 007, json mode, repo local, save path, target spec, tool call, current spec, auto fixes, warn only, save quality, under evidenced, hard blocks, post render, home directory, negative path, user selected, tree thinning, implementation summary, malformed generated memory files, system, spec, kit/022, hybrid, rag, fusion/010, perfect, session, capturing │ ├─────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────┤ │ │ │ Skip session capture │ │ 3 │.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts │ when JSON data is │ │ │ │ provided (Bug 3) │ └─────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────┘ --- Verification 1. ANCHOR preservation: Run generate-context.js on a spec folder and grep output for ANCHOR: grep -c 'ANCHOR' <output-file>.md # Should be >0 (expect ~26 pairs) 2. Trigger phrase replacement: Check frontmatter of generated file: head -10 <output-file>.md # Should NOT contain "memory dashboard", "session summary", "context template" # Should contain session-specific phrases 3. HTML stripping still works: Verify non-ANCHOR HTML comments are still stripped: grep -c '<!-- Template Configuration' <output-file>.md # Should be 0 (template config comments still stripped) 4. Existing tests pass: cd.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit && npm run test --workspace=scripts 5. Quality score impact: The quality scorer at line 1434 checks html (noLeakedTags). Verify the ANCHOR preservation doesn't trigger false positives — ANCHOR tags are valid markdown comments, not "leaked HTML". | Use same spec folder /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Opencode Env/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/022-hybrid-rag-fusion/009-perfect-session-capturing

198947:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-001.md:5840:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/022-hybrid-rag-fusion/009-perfect-session-capturing/scratch/legacy-memory-quarantine/15-03-26_12-25__how-is-used-later-in-the-template-to-match-th.md:649:Implement the following plan: # Plan: Fix Memory File Generation Quality (Root Cause) **Spec folder:** `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/022-hybrid-rag-fusion/009-perfect-session-capturing` ## Context Generated memory files (output of `generate-context.js`) have systemic quality issues: missing ANCHOR tags, generic frontmatter trigger phrases, and cross-session contamination. The user's most recent memory save produced an 893-line file with zero ANCHOR tags despite the template containing 26 of them, and frontmatter trigger_phrases that say "memory dashboard" / "session summary" instead of session-specific content. Additionally, when saving from Claude Code (or any external CLI), the script's session capture detects the active OpenCode editor session instead of the calling session, leading to ALIGNMENT_BLOCK rejections or saving the wrong session's context. Root cause investigation traced these to bugs in `scripts/core/workflow.ts` and `templates/context_template.md`. --- ## Root Cause Analysis ### BUG 1: ANCHOR tags destroyed by HTML comment stripping (CRITICAL) **Location:** `workflow.ts:108,162-179,1360-1364` **Chain of events:** 1. Template `context_template.md` contains 26 ANCHOR tags: `<!-- ANCHOR:id -->` / `<!-- /ANCHOR:id -->` 2. Step 8 (`populateTemplate`) renders the template correctly, anchors intact 3. Step 8.5 calls `stripWorkflowHtmlOutsideCodeFences()` on the rendered output 4. That function uses `WORKFLOW_HTML_COMMENT_RE = //g` (line 108) 5. This regex matches **all** HTML comments, including every ANCHOR tag 6. Line 171: `.replace(WORKFLOW_HTML_COMMENT_RE, '')` — deletes them all 7. Result: 0 ANCHOR tags in output file **Irony:** Line 538 has `extractAnchorIds()` that parses ANCHOR tags — but it runs after they've been destroyed. **Fix:** Modify the HTML comment regex to exclude ANCHOR comments: ```typescript // Before (line 108): const WORKFLOW_HTML_COMMENT_RE = /<!--[\s\S]*?-->/g; // After: const WORKFLOW_HTML_COMMENT_RE = /<!--(?!\s*\/?ANCHOR:)[\s\S]*?-->/g; ``` The negative lookahead `(?!\s*\/?ANCHOR:)` preserves `<!-- ANCHOR:id -->` and `<!-- /ANCHOR:id -->` while still stripping all other HTML comments. ### BUG 2: Frontmatter trigger_phrases are hardcoded (HIGH) **Location:** `templates/context_template.md:4-7` vs `templates/context_template.md:807-811` **Chain of events:** 1. Template frontmatter (lines 4-7) has static values: ```yaml trigger_phrases: - "memory dashboard" - "session summary" - "context template" ``` 2. The ` system spec kit/022 hybrid rag fusion/010 perfect session capturing, dry run, rejection code, no data available, memory_save, json mode, repo local, save path, target spec, tool call, current spec, auto fixes, warn only, save quality, under evidenced, hard blocks, post render, tree thinning, implementation summary, merged-small-files tree-thinning merged small, tree-thinning merged small files, one shared semantic sufficiency, evidence the workflow, cleanup the workflow, keep one ordered native, one ordered native fallback, system, spec, kit/022, hybrid, rag, fusion/010, perfect, session, capturing` mustache variable IS used — but only in the YAML metadata block at line 807-811 (inside the trailing ```` ```yaml ```` code fence) 3. The workflow DOES extract session-specific triggers (`preExtractedTriggers`, line 1231-1269) and passes them as `TRIGGER_PHRASES` to the template (line 1315) 4. But the frontmatter section never references ` system spec kit/022 hybrid rag fusion/010 perfect session capturing, dry run, rejection code, no data available, memory_save, json mode, repo local, save path, target spec, tool call, current spec, auto fixes, warn only, save quality, under evidenced, hard blocks, post render, tree thinning, implementation summary, merged-small-files tree-thinning merged small, tree-thinning merged small files, one shared semantic sufficiency, evidence the workflow, cleanup the workflow, keep one ordered native, one ordered native fallback, system, spec, kit/022, hybrid, rag, fusion/010, perfect, session, capturing` — it uses hardcoded strings **Fix:** Replace the static frontmatter trigger_phrases with the template variable: ```yaml # Before (lines 4-7): trigger_phrases: - "memory dashboard" - "session summary" - "context template" # After: trigger_phrases: {{#TRIGGER_PHRASES}} - "" {{/TRIGGER_PHRASES}}  ``` ### BUG 3: Cross-session contamination — ALREADY FIXED by 010 Spec `009-perfect-session-capturing` (completed 2026-03-15) already addresses this via: - Canonical `.opencode` workspace identity normalization (`workspace-identity.ts`) - Target-spec affinity gate (`spec-affinity.ts`) requiring anchor match beyond workspace - `ALIGNMENT_BLOCK` hard-fail for same-workspace off-spec sessions - `INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT_ABORT` for under-evidenced memories - Safe prompt fallback that drops foreign-spec content **No additional fix needed.** The remaining quality gap (JSON fallback producing lower-quality saves) is by design — JSON mode lacks native session data. The 010 work correctly documents this as an expected limitation. --- ## Files to Modify | # | File | Change | |---|------|--------| | 1 | `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/workflow.ts` | Fix HTML comment regex to preserve ANCHOR tags (line 108) | | 2 | `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/templates/context_template.md` | Replace hardcoded frontmatter trigger_phrases with ` system spec kit/022 hybrid rag fusion/010 perfect session capturing, dry run, rejection code, no data available, memory_save, json mode, repo local, save path, target spec, tool call, current spec, auto fixes, warn only, save quality, under evidenced, hard blocks, post render, tree thinning, implementation summary, merged-small-files tree-thinning merged small, tree-thinning merged small files, one shared semantic sufficiency, evidence the workflow, cleanup the workflow, keep one ordered native, one ordered native fallback, system, spec, kit/022, hybrid, rag, fusion/010, perfect, session, capturing` | --- ## Verification 1. **ANCHOR preservation:** Run `generate-context.js` on a spec folder and grep output for `ANCHOR`: ```bash grep -c 'ANCHOR' .md # Should be >0 (expect ~26 pairs) ``` 2. **Trigger phrase replacement:** Check frontmatter of generated file: ```bash head -10 .md # Should NOT contain "memory dashboard", "session summary", "context template" # Should contain session-specific phrases ``` 3. **HTML stripping still works:** Verify non-ANCHOR HTML comments are still stripped: ```bash grep -c '<!-- Template Configuration' .md # Should be 0 (template config comments still stripped) ``` 4. **Existing tests pass:** ```bash cd.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit && npm run test --workspace=scripts ``` 5. **Quality score impact:** The quality scorer at line 1434 checks `html` (noLeakedTags). Verify the ANCHOR preservation doesn't trigger false positives — ANCHOR tags are valid markdown comments, not "leaked HTML". 199030:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/001-engram-main/research/iterations/iteration-002.md:52:Research Engram's persistent memory architecture, MCP tool design, and session lifecycle patterns to identify practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially for Spec Kit Memory. Focus on how Engram separates agent tools from admin tools, persists session history, stabilizes evolving topics with `topic_key`, and ranks or shortcuts search using SQLite + FTS5. Compare Engram's choices against current Public capabilities and classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`. Prioritize improvements that strengthen session continuity, memory hygiene, search usefulness, and agent-agnostic integration without duplicating systems Public already has.

208694:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:4565:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md:58:Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
208695:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/iterations/iteration-021.md:4566:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md:105:15. Save all outputs inside this phase folder, especially under `research/`. Every meaningful finding must cite exact file paths, say whether the evidence is wrapper-confirmed or README-level, explain why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`, classify the recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, and identify the affected subsystem. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:

Phase C hold or reject: do not add a direct `core=true` shortcut, do not replace Public’s retrieval stack with Mnemosyne, and only pursue an OpenCode-native facade if it delegates to MCP instead of becoming a second authority.

Phase C hold or reject: do not add a direct `core=true` shortcut, do not replace Public’s retrieval stack with Mnemosyne, and only pursue an OpenCode-native facade if it delegates to MCP instead of becoming a second authority.

209340:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:196870:    60	Research MemPalace's raw-verbatim memory architecture, palace taxonomy, wake-up stack, MCP protocol/tooling, hook-driven save flow, and temporal knowledge-graph patterns to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around session continuity, compaction survival, memory hygiene, and agent-facing recall ergonomics. Determine which MemPalace ideas should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`. Keep the analysis honest about what is source-confirmed, what is README-documented, and what is benchmark-claim territory.
209341:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/research/research.md:196920:   110	15. Save all outputs inside `research/`, with `research/research.md` as the canonical report. Every meaningful finding must cite exact file paths, say whether the evidence is `source-confirmed`, `README-documented`, `benchmark-documented`, or mixed, explain why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`, classify the recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, identify the affected subsystem, and note migration or truthfulness risk. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:

210130:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/20--remediation-revalidation/225-remediation-runtime-surface.md:29:| 225 | Runtime remediation, revalidation, and auto-repair workflows | Confirm the live remediation surface blocks unsafe writes, exposes bounded repair paths, and preserves rollback-aware revalidation signals | `Validate the runtime remediation surface for the Spec Kit Memory MCP server. Capture the evidence needed to prove save-time remediation enforces preflight, V-rule, quality-loop, and pre-storage quality gates, that the V-rule bridge resolves the compiled validate-memory-quality runtime at the documented ../../../ path, that operator-facing repair stays confirmation-gated inside memory_health, and that checkpoint plus memory_validate paths still preserve rollback-aware revalidation signals. Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.` | 1) `cd .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server && npx vitest run tests/memory-save-ux-regressions.vitest.ts tests/memory-crud-extended.vitest.ts tests/mcp-input-validation.vitest.ts` 2) `cd .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server && node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const path=require('path'); const p=path.resolve(process.cwd(),'handlers','../../../scripts/dist/memory/validate-memory-quality.js'); console.log(p); console.log(fs.existsSync(p) ? 'exists' : 'missing')"` 3) inspect assertions covering `memory_save` preflight, V-rule disposition handling, quality-loop rejection or repair, and downstream save-quality-gate outcomes 4) inspect assertions covering `memory_health` confirmation-required auto-repair and bounded repair metadata 5) inspect assertions covering `memory_validate` and checkpoint pathways that preserve revalidation or rollback signals | Targeted save, health, and checkpoint suites pass; save-time flows show preflight, validation, and quality-loop enforcement; the V-rule bridge load path resolves successfully; health repair remains confirmation-gated and bounded; and checkpoint or validation paths expose rollback-aware remediation and revalidation signals without contradicting evidence | Test transcript + load-path check output + key assertion output for save-time guards, confirmation gating, repair metadata, and checkpoint or validation signals | PASS if the targeted suites pass, the compiled validator path resolves, and the evidence confirms the remediation surface enforces save-time guards, bounded operator repair, and rollback-aware revalidation behavior end to end | Inspect `mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts`, `mcp_server/lib/validation/preflight.ts`, `mcp_server/handlers/v-rule-bridge.ts`, `mcp_server/handlers/quality-loop.ts`, `mcp_server/lib/validation/save-quality-gate.ts`, `mcp_server/handlers/checkpoints.ts`, and `mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts` if any remediation-stage signal is missing or contradictory |

210807:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:3883:The shipped session-capture pipeline enforces crypto session IDs, atomic batch writes with rollback, contamination filtering, quality abort thresholds, alignment blocking, and configurable pipeline constants. Structured `--stdin` / `--json` input is the only save path; direct positional saves exit non-zero with migration guidance. A semantic sufficiency gate rejects aligned but under-evidenced saves with `INSUFFICIENT_CONTEXT_ABORT`. Rendered memory files preserve ANCHOR comments through post-render cleanup, render session-specific trigger phrases, and accept both camelCase and snake_case save contracts. The shared rendered-memory template contract validates structural output before write/index so routine saves stay structurally clean. Decision confidence, truncated outcome handling, and `git_changed_file_count` follow stable priority chains that respect explicit input values.

211260:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/13--memory-quality-and-indexing/17-outsourced-agent-memory-capture.md:3:description: "Outsourced agent memory capture keeps external CLI save payloads aligned with the current `generate-context.js` contract. 211288:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/16--tooling-and-scripts/149-rendered-memory-template-contract.md:29:| 149 | Rendered memory template contract | Confirm malformed rendered memories fail before write/index and valid rendered output remains validator-clean | `Validate the rendered-memory template contract for memory_save and generate-context. Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.` | 1) Create a sandbox memory missing mandatory anchors or required frontmatter keys 2) `memory_save({ filePath:"<sandbox-file>", dryRun:true })` and verify contract-violation details 3) `memory_save({ filePath:"<sandbox-file>", force:true })` and verify rejection before indexing 4) Run `generate-context.js` with a valid rich JSON payload and verify the rendered output remains validator-clean | Dry-run surfaces template-contract violations; non-dry-run rejects malformed files before index side effects; valid rendered output remains validator-clean | Dry-run/save rejection output + successful render validation evidence | PASS if malformed files are rejected before write/index and valid rendered output remains validator-clean | Inspect `shared/parsing/memory-template-contract.ts`, `scripts/core/workflow.ts`, and `mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts` |
211295:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/13--memory-quality-and-indexing/01-verify-fix-verify-memory-quality-loop.md:29:That means the quality loop can improve structure, but it cannot invent semantic evidence or bless malformed rendered output. A memory that still lacks a specific title, durable file/tool/decision evidence, enough semantic substance, or the required anchor/frontmatter contract remains rejected even after successful auto-fixes.

211667:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/17--governance/123-shared-space-deny-by-default-rollout-phase-6.md:31:| 123 | Shared-space deny-by-default rollout (Phase 6) | Confirm shared-memory spaces require explicit membership and respect rollout/kill-switch controls | `Validate Phase 6 shared-memory rollout controls. Capture the evidence needed to prove Non-members are denied by default; explicit membership grants access; kill switch blocks access immediately even for existing members. Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.` | 1) Create space with `shared_space_upsert({ spaceId, tenantId, name, rolloutEnabled:true, actorUserId:"owner-user-1" })` — exactly one actor identity (actorUserId or actorAgentId) is required; first create auto-grants owner access to the actor 2) Call `shared_memory_status({ userId:"non-member-user-2" })` for a non-member and verify no accessible spaces 3) Attempt shared-space save/search as non-member-user-2 and verify rejection/filtering 4) Grant membership with `shared_space_membership_set({ spaceId, tenantId, subjectType:"user", subjectId:"non-member-user-2", role:"editor", actorUserId:"owner-user-1" })` — actor must be an existing owner, exactly one actor identity required 5) Call `shared_memory_status({ userId:"non-member-user-2" })` and verify shared access succeeds 6) Flip kill switch with `shared_space_upsert({ spaceId, tenantId, name, killSwitch:true, actorUserId:"owner-user-1" })` and verify access is blocked again | Non-members are denied by default; explicit membership grants access; kill switch blocks access immediately even for existing members | Tool outputs for space creation (with actor identity), membership update (with actor identity), status checks, and shared save/search attempts across all three states; verify exactly one actor identity (actorUserId or actorAgentId) is passed on every upsert and membership call | PASS: Non-member denied, member allowed, kill switch blocks again, actor identity required on all mutations; FAIL: Shared access works without membership, kill switch does not stop access, or mutations succeed without actor identity | Verify membership rows in `shared_space_members` → Inspect rollout_enabled and kill_switch flags on `shared_spaces` → Confirm shared-space access path reuses governance checks → Verify actor identity is enforced on `shared_space_upsert` and `shared_space_membership_set` |
211737:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/17--governance/122-governed-ingest-and-scope-isolation-phase-5.md:29:| 122 | Governed ingest and scope isolation (Phase 5) | Confirm governed saves require provenance and scope markers and scoped retrieval blocks cross-actor leakage | `Validate Phase 5 governed ingest and retrieval isolation. Capture the evidence needed to prove agentId,sessionId,provenanceSource,provenanceActor} metadata 3) Query with matching scope and verify hit appears 4) Query with mismatched user/agent or tenant and verify hit is filtered out 5) Review governance_audit rows. Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.` | 1) Attempt `memory_save()` with `tenantId/sessionId` but missing provenance fields and verify rejection 2) Save with full `{tenantId,userId | agentId,sessionId,provenanceSource,provenanceActor}` metadata 3) Query with matching scope and verify hit appears 4) Query with mismatched user/agent or tenant and verify hit is filtered out 5) Review `governance_audit` rows | Missing provenance rejects governed ingest; successful governed ingest persists scope metadata; mismatched scope cannot retrieve the memory; allow/deny decisions are written to `governance_audit` | Save/search outputs + DB query of scoped columns + audit rows showing allow/deny decisions | PASS: Missing provenance rejected, valid governed save succeeds, cross-scope retrieval returns no hit, and audit rows exist; FAIL: Ungoverned save slips through or cross-scope retrieval leaks data |
212081:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/16--tooling-and-scripts/26-core-workflow-infrastructure.md:35:`quality-gates.ts` is the narrow decision point for whether a rendered memory should be indexed at all. It blocks indexing when the file was deduplicated instead of written, when the template contract fails, when semantic sufficiency fails, when the quality score falls below the configured threshold, or when validation disposition requires either write-without-index or full abort. It also formats the semantic-sufficiency rejection string using the shared evidence-count payload so callers can report a consistent failure reason.

212609:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/11--scoring-and-calibration/198-temporal-structural-coherence-scoring.md:29:| 198 | Temporal-structural coherence scoring | Confirm structural penalties, bounded coherence deductions, and immediate retry behavior in the quality loop | `Verify temporal-structural coherence scoring in the quality loop using one well-formed control memory and flawed variants. Submit variants that are too short or missing headings, include a future-dated completion claim, and include self-referential or unresolved causal links. Confirm the coherence breakdown applies the expected structural checks and bounded penalties, and verify that low-coherence content is rejected or downgraded while the quality loop retry cycle stays immediate and bounded. Capture the score breakdown and return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.` | 1) Prepare a well-formed control memory plus flawed variants covering short or heading-less content, future-dated completion claims, and broken causal-link metadata 2) Run the quality loop or ingest path for each case 3) Inspect the coherence score breakdown and quality-loop decision for control vs flawed variants 4) Confirm penalties apply to temporal and causal-link issues without inventing broader chronology logic 5) For an auto-fixable case, capture the retry cycle and confirm retries occur immediately with no backoff and stop within the configured limit | Structural checks evaluate content presence, minimum length, and headings; future-dated completion claims reduce coherence; self-referential or unresolved causal links reduce coherence; low-coherence variants are rejected or downgraded; retry attempts are immediate and bounded | Quality-loop transcript, coherence score breakdown, control-vs-variant results, and retry timing evidence | PASS: coherence penalties appear for the flawed variants, low-quality cases are blocked or downgraded, and retry timing stays immediate and bounded; FAIL: penalties are missing, weak content passes without explanation, or retries show unexpected backoff or unbounded looping | Verify control content satisfies structural checks -> Inspect coherence penalty branches for future-dated claims and causal links -> Confirm rejection threshold or downgrade path -> Check retry loop configuration and attempt count -> Ensure broader chronology analysis was not assumed in the verdict |

212727:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:44:  inputSchema: { type: 'object', additionalProperties: false, properties: { input: { type: 'string', minLength: 1, description: 'The query, prompt, or context description (required)' }, mode: { type: 'string', enum: ['auto', 'quick', 'deep', 'focused', 'resume'], default: 'auto', description: 'Context retrieval mode: auto (detect intent), quick (fast triggers), deep (comprehensive search), focused (intent-optimized), resume (session recovery)' }, intent: { type: 'string', enum: ['add_feature', 'fix_bug', 'refactor', 'security_audit', 'understand', 'find_spec', 'find_decision'], description: 'Explicit task intent. If not provided and mode=auto, intent is auto-detected from input.' }, specFolder: { type: 'string', description: 'Limit context to specific spec folder' }, tenantId: { type: 'string', description: 'Tenant boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, userId: { type: 'string', description: 'User boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, agentId: { type: 'string', description: 'Agent boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, sharedSpaceId: { type: 'string', description: 'Shared-space boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, limit: { type: 'number', minimum: 1, maximum: 100, description: 'Maximum results (mode-specific defaults apply)' }, sessionId: { type: 'string', description: 'Optional server-issued session identifier for working-memory continuity. When provided, it must match an existing server-managed session or the call is rejected. Omit it to let the server generate a new session for this request.' }, enableDedup: { type: 'boolean', default: true, description: 'Enable session deduplication' }, includeContent: { type: 'boolean', default: false, description: 'Include full file content in results' }, includeTrace: { type: 'boolean', default: false, description: 'Include provenance-rich trace data (scores, source, trace) in results when underlying memory_search is called' }, tokenUsage: { type: 'number', minimum: 0.0, maximum: 1.0, description: "Optional caller token usage ratio (0.0-1.0)" }, anchors: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'Filter content to specific anchors (e.g., ["state", "next-steps"] for resume mode)' }, profile: { type: 'string', enum: ['quick', 'research', 'resume', 'debug'], description: 'Optional response profile formatter. Returns a reduced or mode-aware response shape when profile formatting is enabled.' } }, required: ['input'] },

217907:## Findings
217910:### Finding 1: Public-native pre-compaction preservation is the highest-value MemPalace transfer, but only with structured save guardrails
217914:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
217917:### Finding 2: Status-as-behavior-bootstrap is cheap, effective, and safer in Public as a short hint layer
217921:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
217924:### Finding 3: MemPalace’s real verbatim win is evidence retention, not literal “store everything” ingestion
217928:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
217931:### Finding 4: The layered wake-up interface is reusable, but MemPalace’s current L1 salience engine is weaker than its product story
217935:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
217938:### Finding 5: A separate temporal fact plane is still the biggest upside item, but it needs stronger authority controls than MemPalace has today
217942:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
217945:### Finding 6: Full palace taxonomy is still the easiest thing to overbuild and the hardest thing to keep honest
217949:- **Recommendation**: reject
217978:- New information ratio: 0.82
217986:## Recommended Next Focus

219175:## Recommended Next Focus
219189:Research MemPalace's raw-verbatim memory architecture, palace taxonomy, wake-up stack, MCP protocol/tooling, hook-driven save flow, and temporal knowledge-graph patterns to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around session continuity, compaction survival, memory hygiene, and agent-facing recall ergonomics. Determine which MemPalace ideas should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`. Keep the analysis honest about what is source-confirmed, what is README-documented, and what is benchmark-claim territory.
219239:15. Save all outputs inside `research/`, with `research/research.md` as the canonical report. Every meaningful finding must cite exact file paths, say whether the evidence is `source-confirmed`, `README-documented`, `benchmark-documented`, or mixed, explain why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`, classify the recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, identify the affected subsystem, and note migration or truthfulness risk. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:

223399:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/06--analysis/01-causal-edge-creation-memorycausallink.md:22:Edge bounds are enforced at insert time. Auto-generated edges (those with `created_by='auto'`) are rejected when a node already has 20 edges (`MAX_EDGES_PER_NODE`) and clamped to a maximum strength of 0.5 (`MAX_AUTO_STRENGTH`). Every strength modification is logged to a `weight_history` table recording old strength, new strength, who changed it, when and why. The `created_by` and `last_accessed` fields on each edge track provenance and usage patterns.
223693:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:44:  inputSchema: { type: 'object', additionalProperties: false, properties: { input: { type: 'string', minLength: 1, description: 'The query, prompt, or context description (required)' }, mode: { type: 'string', enum: ['auto', 'quick', 'deep', 'focused', 'resume'], default: 'auto', description: 'Context retrieval mode: auto (detect intent), quick (fast triggers), deep (comprehensive search), focused (intent-optimized), resume (session recovery)' }, intent: { type: 'string', enum: ['add_feature', 'fix_bug', 'refactor', 'security_audit', 'understand', 'find_spec', 'find_decision'], description: 'Explicit task intent. If not provided and mode=auto, intent is auto-detected from input.' }, specFolder: { type: 'string', description: 'Limit context to specific spec folder' }, tenantId: { type: 'string', description: 'Tenant boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, userId: { type: 'string', description: 'User boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, agentId: { type: 'string', description: 'Agent boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, sharedSpaceId: { type: 'string', description: 'Shared-space boundary for governed retrieval when memory_context routes to memory_search.' }, limit: { type: 'number', minimum: 1, maximum: 100, description: 'Maximum results (mode-specific defaults apply)' }, sessionId: { type: 'string', description: 'Optional server-issued session identifier for working-memory continuity. When provided, it must match an existing server-managed session or the call is rejected. Omit it to let the server generate a new session for this request.' }, enableDedup: { type: 'boolean', default: true, description: 'Enable session deduplication' }, includeContent: { type: 'boolean', default: false, description: 'Include full file content in results' }, includeTrace: { type: 'boolean', default: false, description: 'Include provenance-rich trace data (scores, source, trace) in results when underlying memory_search is called' }, tokenUsage: { type: 'number', minimum: 0.0, maximum: 1.0, description: "Optional caller token usage ratio (0.0-1.0)" }, anchors: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'Filter content to specific anchors (e.g., ["state", "next-steps"] for resume mode)' }, profile: { type: 'string', enum: ['quick', 'research', 'resume', 'debug'], description: 'Optional response profile formatter. Returns a reduced or mode-aware response shape when profile formatting is enabled.' } }, required: ['input'] },

Capture the evidence needed to prove governed saves require provenance metadata; ephemeral retention without deleteAfter is rejected; matching scope can read the row; mismatched user/agent or tenant is filtered out; governance_audit rows are recorded; and a simulated governance post-step failure does not leave behind a row with missing governance fields. Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.`
225639:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/16--tooling-and-scripts/237-spec-lifecycle-automation.md:29:| 237 | Spec Lifecycle Automation | Confirm lifecycle tool availability across recommendation, upgrade, completeness, completion, and archival surfaces | `Validate the spec lifecycle automation toolchain. Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.` | 1) `bash .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/recommend-level.sh --help` 2) `bash .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/test-upgrade-level.sh` 3) `bash .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/calculate-completeness.sh .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/test-fixtures/063-template-compliant-level3 --json` 4) `bash .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/check-completion.sh .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/test-fixtures/063-template-compliant-level3 --json` 5) `bash .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/archive.sh --help` | Recommendation and archive help text is available; upgrade-level regression suite passes; completeness JSON emits percentage-style data; completion status is returned for the fixture | Help output, upgrade test transcript, completeness JSON, and completion JSON | PASS if the lifecycle entrypoints are reachable and the upgrade/completion signals match the documented toolchain behavior | Inspect `scripts/spec/recommend-level.sh`, `scripts/tests/test-upgrade-level.sh`, `scripts/spec/calculate-completeness.sh`, and `scripts/spec/archive.sh` if a lifecycle stage is missing or inconsistent |
225718:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/13--memory-quality-and-indexing/165-assistive-reconsolidation-speckit-assistive-reconsolidation.md:20:- Expected signals: similarity >= 0.96 returns 'auto_merge' and archives the older row; 0.88 <= similarity < 0.96 returns 'review' with AssistiveRecommendation logged; similarity < 0.88 returns 'keep_separate'; review tier produces classification (supersede/complement/keep_separate) without destructive action; companion merge guard aborts stale merge attempts with `predecessor_changed` or `predecessor_gone`

225762:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/11--scoring-and-calibration/026-classification-based-decay-tm-03.md:29:| 026 | Classification-based decay (TM-03) | Confirm class+tier decay matrix and zero-half-life rejection | `Verify TM-03 classification-based decay. Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.` | 1) Load the documented class/tier decay matrix 2) Validate representative class+tier outputs against the matrix 3) Submit a config with `halfLifeDays: 0` 4) Confirm validation rejects it with the positive-number-or-null error | Decay multipliers differ by classification and tier; matrix values match documented configuration; zero half-life config is rejected with the positive-number-or-null error | Scoring path output with per-class/tier decay multipliers + config-validation output for `halfLifeDays: 0` | PASS: Each class+tier combination produces the documented multiplier, and zero half-life config is rejected up front; FAIL: Multipliers missing, mismatch the matrix, or `halfLifeDays: 0` passes validation | Verify decay matrix configuration → Check classification assignment → Inspect `validateHalfLifeConfig()` in `memory-types.ts` → Confirm the positive-number-or-null guard |
225785:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/04--maintenance/014-workspace-scanning-and-indexing-memory-index-scan.md:19:- Prompt: `Run the incremental scan flow and the targeted atomic lease checks for memory_index_scan. Capture the evidence needed to prove scan summary and updated index state, that spec documents remain indexed in warn-only quality mode, and that scan leases reserve scan_started_at atomically, reject overlapping fresh scans, expire stale crashed-scan leases, and only move to last_index_scan on completion. Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.`
225786:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/04--maintenance/014-workspace-scanning-and-indexing-memory-index-scan.md:20:- Expected signals: Scan summary, updated index state, spec-doc warn-only indexing behavior, atomic lease reservation, rejection wait time, stale-lease expiry, and completion handoff to `last_index_scan`
225788:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/04--maintenance/014-workspace-scanning-and-indexing-memory-index-scan.md:30:| EX-014 | Workspace scanning and indexing (memory_index_scan) | Atomic lease acquisition reserves the scan slot before work begins | `Run the targeted lease-acquisition validation. Capture the evidence needed to prove a fresh memory_index_scan reservation writes scan_started_at inside the lease transaction before the handler proceeds. Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.` | `cd .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server` -> `npm test -- --run tests/db-state.vitest.ts -t "acquires scan lease once and rejects a concurrent fresh lease"` | First lease acquisition succeeds and the lease timestamp is reserved immediately | Targeted test transcript showing the first acquisition passed before the concurrent check | PASS if the targeted test passes and the first reservation remains an acquired lease | Inspect `core/db-state.ts` transaction flow around `acquireIndexScanLease()` if the first acquisition no longer succeeds |
225789:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/04--maintenance/014-workspace-scanning-and-indexing-memory-index-scan.md:31:| EX-014 | Workspace scanning and indexing (memory_index_scan) | Overlapping fresh scans are rejected with an active-lease wait time | `Run the targeted active-lease rejection validation. Capture the evidence needed to prove a second fresh memory_index_scan attempt is rejected while the first lease is still active, with a positive wait time rather than a second reservation. Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.` | `cd .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server` -> `npm test -- --run tests/db-state.vitest.ts -t "acquires scan lease once and rejects a concurrent fresh lease"` | Second lease attempt is rejected with `reason: 'lease_active'` and a positive wait time | Targeted test transcript showing the second attempt failed for `lease_active` | PASS if the targeted test passes and the rejection path still reports `reason: 'lease_active'` with `waitSeconds > 0` | Inspect `core/db-state.ts` lease-activity branch and `handlers/memory-index.ts` E429 details if concurrent scans start slipping through |
225829:.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/17--governance/123-shared-space-deny-by-default-rollout-phase-6.md:31:| 123 | Shared-space deny-by-default rollout (Phase 6) | Confirm shared-memory spaces require explicit membership and respect rollout/kill-switch controls | `Validate Phase 6 shared-memory rollout controls. Capture the evidence needed to prove Non-members are denied by default; explicit membership grants access; kill switch blocks access immediately even for existing members. Return a concise user-facing pass/fail verdict with the main reason.` | 1) Create space with `shared_space_upsert({ spaceId, tenantId, name, rolloutEnabled:true, actorUserId:"owner-user-1" })` — exactly one actor identity (actorUserId or actorAgentId) is required; first create auto-grants owner access to the actor 2) Call `shared_memory_status({ userId:"non-member-user-2" })` for a non-member and verify no accessible spaces 3) Attempt shared-space save/search as non-member-user-2 and verify rejection/filtering 4) Grant membership with `shared_space_membership_set({ spaceId, tenantId, subjectType:"user", subjectId:"non-member-user-2", role:"editor", actorUserId:"owner-user-1" })` — actor must be an existing owner, exactly one actor identity required 5) Call `shared_memory_status({ userId:"non-member-user-2" })` and verify shared access succeeds 6) Flip kill switch with `shared_space_upsert({ spaceId, tenantId, name, killSwitch:true, actorUserId:"owner-user-1" })` and verify access is blocked again | Non-members are denied by default; explicit membership grants access; kill switch blocks access immediately even for existing members | Tool outputs for space creation (with actor identity), membership update (with actor identity), status checks, and shared save/search attempts across all three states; verify exactly one actor identity (actorUserId or actorAgentId) is passed on every upsert and membership call | PASS: Non-member denied, member allowed, kill switch blocks again, actor identity required on all mutations; FAIL: Shared access works without membership, kill switch does not stop access, or mutations succeed without actor identity | Verify membership rows in `shared_space_members` → Inspect rollout_enabled and kill_switch flags on `shared_spaces` → Confirm shared-space access path reuses governance checks → Verify actor identity is enforced on `shared_space_upsert` and `shared_space_membership_set` |

230192:## Findings
230193:### Finding 1: MemPalace handles changing truth with explicit time windows, not retrieval decay
230198:- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
230201:### Finding 2: The primary drawer store does not really forget; aging is mostly avoided, not modeled
230206:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
230209:### Finding 3: MemPalace’s wake-up layer claims recency, but the implementation only uses importance
230214:- **Recommendation**: reject direct copy
230217:### Finding 4: Hook timing protects against context loss, but it is not a memory-decay strategy
230222:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
230225:### Finding 5: Diary entries provide episodic recency, but remain append-only history
230230:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
230233:### Finding 6: Public already has the stronger decay stack; the missing temporal piece is fact invalidation
230238:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
230242:- New information ratio: 0.38
230246:## Recommended Next Focus

230406:Research MemPalace's raw-verbatim memory architecture, palace taxonomy, wake-up stack, MCP protocol/tooling, hook-driven save flow, and temporal knowledge-graph patterns to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around session continuity, compaction survival, memory hygiene, and agent-facing recall ergonomics. Determine which MemPalace ideas should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`. Keep the analysis honest about what is source-confirmed, what is README-documented, and what is benchmark-claim territory.
230456:15. Save all outputs inside `research/`, with `research/research.md` as the canonical report. Every meaningful finding must cite exact file paths, say whether the evidence is `source-confirmed`, `README-documented`, `benchmark-documented`, or mixed, explain why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`, classify the recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, identify the affected subsystem, and note migration or truthfulness risk. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:

261835:## Findings
261836:### Finding 1: MemPalace’s shipped retrieval is semantic-first; most lexical gains live in benchmark code, not the product path
261840:- **Recommendation**: `reject`
261843:### Finding 2: Lexical beats semantic on exact anchors: quoted phrases, names, slugs, and artifact lookups
261847:- **Recommendation**: `adopt now`
261850:### Finding 3: Semantic beats lexical for paraphrase-heavy recall, open-ended continuity, and “why” questions
261854:- **Recommendation**: `adopt now`
261857:### Finding 4: The right hybrid strategy is routed fusion, not equal-weight “run everything” retrieval
261861:- **Recommendation**: `adopt now`
261864:### Finding 5: Taxonomy and metadata filters are narrowing tools, not substitutes for lexical or semantic retrieval
261868:- **Recommendation**: `adopt now`
261872:- New information ratio: 0.41

279333:## Recommended Next Focus
282598:The core pattern is getting clearer: MemPalace separates verbatim drawers, metadata-derived palace navigation, and a second fact store with explicit invalidation. I’m drilling into the write path, chunking/routing, hook blocking behavior, and Public’s startup/save contract now so the adopt/reject calls stay specific.
284418:The decision set is stable now: MemPalace’s strongest ideas are protocol bootstrapping, metadata-guided narrowing, auditability, and a clean fact-store split; its weakest bets are forced AI-driven save hooks and AAAK-heavy compression claims. I’m wrapping this into the iteration report with direct adopt/prototype/reject calls for Public.
284442:ARCHITECTURE DECISION RECORD: Document the key architectural decisions this system made and why. Which decisions should we adopt and which should we reject?
284444:## Findings
284445:### Finding 1: Keep verbatim evidence as the authority, then add narrower retrieval views on top
284449:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
284452:### Finding 2: Taxonomy is useful as a metadata routing overlay, not as a distinct retrieval engine
284456:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
284459:### Finding 3: Status should teach the agent how to use memory, not just report health
284463:- **Recommendation**: adopt now
284466:### Finding 4: A cheap wake-up stack is reusable, but not as a replacement for Public’s stronger resume/bootstrap flow
284470:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
284473:### Finding 5: Keep mutable facts in a separate temporal store with explicit invalidation
284477:- **Recommendation**: prototype later
284480:### Finding 6: Strong forensic surfaces are worth copying; forced hook-blocking saves are not
284484:- **Recommendation**: reject
284487:### Finding 7: AAAK-heavy compression is an experimental side lane, not an architectural foundation
284491:- **Recommendation**: reject
284495:- New information ratio: 0.36
284497:## Recommended Next Focus
284498:Convert this ADR into a final adoption matrix for iteration 30: `adopt now` should be protocol bootstrap, bounded verbatim evidence retention, and local audit/repair surfaces; `prototype later` should be a temporal fact sidecar and optional wake-up brief; `reject` should remain forced hook-blocking saves and AAAK-centered compression as core architecture.
